steps.title: Mark the body axis (pencil)
steps.description: Lightly sketch a short vertical line — this is the figure's entire height. At this distance the person is small, so keep the mark genuinely small. The scale you set now is the whole lesson.

steps.title: Place the head and joints (pencil)
steps.description: Add a tiny oval at the top of the axis for the head. Place a small horizontal tick about a third of the way down for the shoulders, another just past the halfway point for the hips, and two dots at the very bottom for the feet.

steps.title: Rough in the limbs (pencil)
steps.description: Sketch the legs as two short lines dropping from the hip tick to the foot dots. Add a hint of arms — just two faint strokes hanging from the shoulders. At this scale one stroke per limb is plenty.

steps.title: Switch to ink and commit the silhouette
steps.description: Switch to ink. Trace the head oval, then draw the torso as a single narrow stroke from shoulders to hips. Keep the line light at this scale — a heavy stroke will look like a fence post, not a person.

steps.title: Ink the limbs (ink)
steps.description: Add the legs and a suggestion of arms — two or three short strokes each. Stop there. Distance simplifies everything; any more marks and the figure stops reading as far away.
